meta.title: BMW X1 18d Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW X1 18d engine — N47 and B47. E84, F48 and U11 generations, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X1 18d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned X1 18d engine typically costs £2,800–£5,000 supply only depending on generation, with the B47 currently sitting at £3,000–£5,500. Fitting adds £900–£1,400. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X1 18d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the generation. Pre-2017 cars with the N47 have the well-documented rear-chain risk (★★★☆☆). 2017+ cars with the B47 have the chain relocated to the front (★★★★☆), eliminating the specific N47 failure mode. The B47 is considered meaningfully more reliable.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X1 18d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain wear on N47-engined cars (pre-2017), B47 timing chain tensioner wear (2017+), and an EGR cooler coolant leak affecting both engines. The B47 is significantly more reliable than the N47.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW X1 18d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept F48 X1 18d worth £6,000–£10,000, a £3,800 recon is 38–63% of the car's value — assess carefully. For a U11 worth £12,000+, the same recon is 32% — more justifiable. Always assess the rest of the car before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X1 18d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: E84 and F48 pre-2017 is N47, F48 2017+ and U11 is B47. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N47 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£650–£1,800) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (£2,800–£5,000 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

eraMap.title: The X1 18d THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.reliability: ★★★☆☆ [BMW-VERIFIED — inherited from Model Page & E90 Generation Page]
rows.eraNote: First-generation X1 18d — N47 with rear-chain risk; the defining issue of this era.
The N47-to-B47 transition year — 2017+ F48 cars have the front-mounted chain.
Facelift F48 — B47 only; front-mounted chain, meaningfully improved reliability.
The latest X1 18d — B47 with 48V mild-hybrid on later examples; the most refined 18d yet.

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an X1 18d engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW X1 18d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches the fault before it becomes catastrophic
Straightforward fix if caught before overheating
If caught early — far cheaper than a new engine
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair
Preventative — regular motorway runs are the best prevention

buyingChecks.title: Buying This X1 18d? Check These First
buyIf: * 2017+ B47 if you want to avoid the N47 chain risk entirely
* Documented timing-chain history if buying a pre-2017 N47
* Confirmed EGR recall completion on any X1 18d
* Full service history with sub-15,000km oil intervals
avoidIf: * Pre-2017 N47 with cold-start rattle and no chain history
* EGR recall not completed on any X1 18d
* Poor service history on any generation
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis, regardless of generation

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The X1 18d is the core compact SUV diesel — and the engine choice determines your ownership experience. 2017+ B47 cars are meaningfully more reliable than the earlier N47. Buy on documented history, and if you're looking at a pre-2017 car, demand chain-history proof before you commit.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X1 18d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for 1–30 seconds of rattle (timing chain warning on N47)
* Service History — Including which engine era (N47 for pre-2017, B47 for 2017+) — see the Era Map above
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate EGR coolant leak
* Turbo Boost — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or grinding?
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate wear on all diesel eras
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.label: Most Common Failure
items.value: N47 rear timing chain — approximately 60% of pre-2017 18d enquiries [PATTERN DATA]

items.label: Peak Enquiry Season
